description: Musical Audio Enhancement & Online Ranking — EDM/DnB procedural music with chord pad, arpeggio, bass line, musical SFX, and online score ranking via webhook.

userStories.title: Harmonic chord pad (supersaw)
userStories.description: As a player, I want a harmonic chord pad playing during gameplay so that the audio feels musical and immersive like an EDM track.
acceptanceCriteria: Pad synthesized with 3-4 simultaneous notes forming chords (Am → F → C → G progression in A minor)
Chord progression loops (4-8 bars) synchronized to the existing BPM beat scheduler
'Supersaw' timbre using 3 detuned sawtooth oscillators per note with a low-pass filter
Filter cutoff and volume increase gradually with scrollSpeed (200→800 px/s)
Smooth fade-in when entering playingState, fade-out on explosion/game over (0.3s)
Frequencies chosen to not clash with engine drone (engine ~60-180Hz, pad notes above 200Hz)
Pad starts/stops via playingState onEnter/onExit, follows same pattern as engine/road
All oscillators connect through AudioManager.masterGain
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: continuous harmonic pad audible during gameplay

userStories.title: Procedural arpeggio melody
userStories.description: As a player, I want a procedural arpeggio melody that evolves with game speed so that the music feels dynamic and exciting like a DnB track.
acceptanceCriteria: Arpeggiator plays notes from A minor pentatonic scale (A, C, D, E, G) in sync with the beat scheduler
Note speed adapts to scrollSpeed: slow (~200 px/s) = quarter notes (1/4), fast (~800 px/s) = sixteenth notes (1/16)
Notes derived from current chord of the pad (US-001) to maintain harmony
Lead synth timbre: square wave with slight detune + band-pass filter
Every 8 bars, arpeggio pattern changes subtly (inversion or octave shift)
Volume lower than beat and pad (gain ~0.15) so it does not dominate the mix
Arpeggiator uses AudioContext.currentTime scheduling, not setTimeout
Starts/stops with playingState onEnter/onExit
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: arpeggio melody audible and note speed changes with speed

userStories.title: Synchronized DnB bass line
userStories.description: As a player, I want a rhythmic bass line synced with the beat so that the music has the driving low-end energy of EDM/DnB.
acceptanceCriteria: Bass synth uses filtered sawtooth (low-pass ~200Hz) playing root notes of current chord
DnB rhythmic pattern: note on beat 1, syncopated ghost notes on beats 2.5 and 3.5
Frequency range 40-100Hz with short attack (<0.01s) and medium decay (~0.15s) envelope
Sidechain-like duck: bass gain briefly reduces (to 0.3x) for 0.05s when kick drum fires
Synchronized with existing beat scheduler via AudioContext.currentTime
Starts/stops with playingState onEnter/onExit
Volume in mix: below kick/hat, above pad (gain ~0.3)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: bass line audible and complements the kick

userStories.title: Musical pickup SFX using current scale
userStories.description: As a player, I want pickup sounds to be musical notes that harmonize with the current music so that collecting items feels satisfying and part of the soundtrack.
acceptanceCriteria: Coin pickup: plays a note from A minor pentatonic scale; each consecutive coin in cluster steps up one scale degree (ascending arpeggio)
Fuel pickup: quick major triad chord (3 simultaneous notes, ~0.15s) derived from current chord root
Nitro pickup: ascending sweep ending on the tonic (A) of the current scale
Shield pickup: two notes a perfect 5th apart from the current scale with tremolo (LFO amplitude mod at ~8Hz)
Near miss: short chromatic grace note (one semitone below current tonic, quick 0.05s)
Combo multiplier: power chord (root + 5th) where root pitch rises with combo level
All SFX still trigger at same code locations as before — only frequencies change to use musical scale
SFX remain harmonious when music is playing (no atonal clashes)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: pickups sound musical and in harmony with the track

userStories.title: Musical state transitions
userStories.description: As a player, I want smooth musical transitions between game states so that the audio experience feels polished and cinematic.
acceptanceCriteria: Title → Playing: 1s riser (white noise sweep ascending + volume crescendo) plays before beat drops
Collision/Explosion: all musical elements (pad, arpeggio, bass) stop with 'tape stop' effect (pitch drops rapidly to ~20% over 0.3s then silence)
Game Over: existing somber drone enhanced — add a diminished minor chord resolving slowly over 2s
Retry (game over → playing): short drum roll fill (4 rapid kicks over 0.3s) before beat restarts
All transitions use AudioContext.currentTime scheduling for precise timing
No clicks, pops, or silent gaps during transitions
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: all state transitions have distinct audible musical changes

userStories.title: Player name input on Game Over screen
userStories.description: As a player, I want to enter my name on the Game Over screen so that my score can be identified in the ranking.
acceptanceCriteria: HTML text input element appears as overlay above canvas on gameOverState (positioned absolutely over canvas)
Placeholder text 'Enter your name' with 15 character limit (maxlength attribute)
Keyboard input into field does not trigger game controls (arrows, space, enter for retry)
Player name saved to localStorage key 'roadRushPlayerName' and pre-filled on subsequent visits
Submit button labeled 'Submit Score' below the input field
Pressing Enter in the name input submits the score (same as clicking Submit Score button)
Validation: name must be at least 2 characters; show inline error 'Name too short' if not
Input and button hidden/removed when transitioning out of gameOverState
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: text input visible on game over, typing works, name persists in localStorage

userStories.title: Score submission via webhook POST
userStories.description: As a player, I want my score automatically submitted to the online ranking so that I can compete with other players.
acceptanceCriteria: POST to https://n8n.ai-solutions.startse.com/webhook/e6c46e71-f564-4e8b-b6bd-041ca8f012e0 on Submit Score
Payload JSON: { name, score, distance, time, causeOfDeath, coinsCollected }
Track coinsCollected in gameState (increment on each coin pickup, reset in resetGameState)
causeOfDeath is either 'collision' or 'fuel_empty' depending on how game ended
Visual feedback: Submit button shows 'Sending...' while awaiting response
Success state: button replaced with 'Score submitted! ✓' (non-clickable)
Error state: shows 'Failed to submit. Try again' with button re-enabled for retry
Submit button disabled after successful submission to prevent duplicates
Game retry (Enter key) still works even if submission fails or is pending
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: score POST succeeds and feedback updates correctly

userStories.title: Ranking display via webhook GET
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see the top scores from all players on the Game Over screen so that I know how I compare.
acceptanceCriteria: GET to same webhook endpoint to fetch ranking after score submission (or in parallel)
Ranking rendered on canvas in gameOverState below the stats section
Displays: rank position, player name, score, distance — for top 10 entries visible initially
Scroll up/down with arrow keys to see more entries (if more than 10)
Current player's entry highlighted with a different color (e.g., yellow) based on name match
Loading state: shows 'Loading ranking...' with animated dots while fetching
Error state: shows 'Could not load ranking' with a retry option
Handles empty or malformed GET response gracefully (no crash, shows 'No scores yet')
Ranking list limited to top 50 entries displayed maximum
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: ranking table visible on game over with correct data

userStories.title: Audio mix and master bus balancing
userStories.description: As a player, I want the audio mix to be well-balanced so that no element overpowers another and the overall experience is pleasant.
acceptanceCriteria: Volume hierarchy enforced: Beat (kick 0.35/hat 0.12) > Bass (~0.30) > Pad (~0.20) > Arpeggio (~0.15) > SFX (0.25-0.35) > Engine/Road (~0.10 when music playing)
Crash/explosion SFX duck all musical elements to 0.3x gain for 0.5s using setTargetAtTime
Add a DynamicsCompressorNode on masterGain → destination as a limiter (threshold -6dB, ratio 10:1, knee 3dB)
Engine drone volume reduced to ~0.1 (from current level) when musical elements (pad/bass/arpeggio) are active
All new elements added in US-001–US-005 verified to go through AudioManager.masterGain (mute works)
No clipping or distortion with all elements playing simultaneously
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: full mix sounds balanced with no dominant or inaudible elements
